_comment: Notable dates and events of the day that can be mentioned in the podcast when they align with the daily theme. Each entry has theme_tags (matching weekday theme indices) so the system surfaces only relevant dates. These are secondary — they don't drive PSA org selection, they add color and context to the episode.

dates.name: World Radio Day
dates.note: Celebrates radio as a medium for education and free speech — a natural fit for a podcast about community media.

dates.name: World Poetry Day
dates.note: UNESCO day celebrating poetry's role in cultural expression and linguistic diversity.

dates.name: International Dance Day
dates.note: Celebrates dance as a form of cultural expression — relevant to Cariboo arts programming.

dates.name: World Press Freedom Day
dates.note: A reminder of the importance of local journalism and free press in rural communities.

dates.name: World Music Day (Fete de la Musique)
dates.note: Global celebration of music — coincides with the longest day of the year and the Cariboo's summer arts season.

dates.name: International Day of Rural Women
dates.note: Recognizes the critical role of rural women in agriculture, food security, and community leadership.

dates.name: World Soil Day
dates.note: Raises awareness of soil health and sustainable land management — central to Cariboo ranching and farming.

dates.name: Agriculture Day in Canada
dates.note: Celebrating Canadian agriculture and the people who grow our food — Cariboo ranchers and farmers included.

dates.name: International Day of Forests
dates.note: UN day highlighting the importance of forests to people and the planet — forestry is a cornerstone of the Cariboo economy.

dates.name: World Bee Day
dates.note: Pollinators are essential to Cariboo agriculture and wild ecosystems alike.

dates.name: Open Data Day
dates.note: Global celebration of open data — relevant to civic transparency and community tech governance.

dates.name: International Day of Democracy
dates.note: A day to reflect on civic participation, local governance, and community engagement.

dates.name: World Cities Day
dates.note: UN day on sustainable urbanization — what does that mean for small cities like Williams Lake?

dates.name: Civic Holiday (BC Day)
dates.note: BC Day — a chance to celebrate what makes British Columbia and the Cariboo special.

dates.name: National Indigenous Languages Day
dates.note: Celebrates Indigenous language revitalization — Secwepemctsin, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh language programs in the Cariboo.

dates.name: International Day of the World's Indigenous Peoples
dates.note: UN day recognizing Indigenous peoples' rights, cultures, and contributions worldwide.

dates.name: Treaty Day / Douglas Treaties anniversary
dates.note: A day to reflect on treaty relationships and the ongoing work of reconciliation in BC.

dates.name: Moose Hide Campaign Day
dates.note: Indigenous-led campaign calling on men and boys to stand against violence toward women and children.

dates.name: World Migratory Bird Day
dates.note: Celebrates migratory birds and their habitats — Scout Island and the Cariboo wetlands are critical stopover points.

dates.name: World Environment Day
dates.note: UN day for environmental awareness — relevant to Cariboo conservation, wildfire resilience, and land stewardship.

dates.name: International Mountain Day
dates.note: Celebrates mountain ecosystems and communities — the Cariboo and Chilcotin ranges are our backyard.

dates.name: World Rivers Day
dates.note: Celebrates waterways worldwide — the Fraser, Chilcotin, and Quesnel rivers are the arteries of the Cariboo.

dates.name: World Wildlife Day
dates.note: UN day celebrating wild animals and plants — the Cariboo is home to caribou, grizzly, salmon, and diverse ecosystems.

dates.name: National Trails Day
dates.note: Celebrating trails and the people who build and maintain them — from Gold Rush Trail to First Journey Trails.

dates.name: World Wildfire Community Preparedness Day
dates.note: A day to prepare for wildfire season — the Cariboo knows this reality all too well after 2017 and 2023.

dates.name: International Day of Cooperatives
dates.note: Celebrates the cooperative movement — relevant to community-owned infrastructure and rural economic resilience.

dates.name: World Telecommunications Day
dates.note: ITU day on connectivity and digital inclusion — broadband access remains a key challenge in rural BC.

dates.name: Global Recycling Day
dates.note: Raises awareness of recycling and waste reduction — relevant to sustainability efforts in the Cariboo.

dates.name: World Humanitarian Day
dates.note: Honours humanitarian workers — a moment to recognize the volunteers and organizations that serve the Cariboo.

dates.name: Rural Health Day
dates.note: National Rural Health Day — spotlighting healthcare access challenges and innovations in rural communities like ours.

dates.name: World Mental Health Day
dates.note: Global day for mental health awareness and advocacy — CMHA Cariboo Chilcotin is a local lifeline.

dates.name: International Volunteer Day
dates.note: Recognizes volunteers worldwide — Cariboo organizations from SAR to hospice run on volunteer power.

dates.name: World Water Day
dates.note: UN day on water and sanitation — Cariboo watersheds face pressures from development, drought, and climate change.

dates.name: Small Business Saturday
dates.note: Celebrating small and local businesses — the backbone of Cariboo towns.

dates.name: International Youth Day
dates.note: Celebrates young people and their role in community — rural youth retention is a key Cariboo challenge.

dates.name: World Science Day for Peace and Development
dates.note: UNESCO day celebrating the role of science in society and the importance of scientific literacy — a natural fit for a Sunday of wonder and discovery.

dates.name: Darwin Day
dates.note: Marks Charles Darwin's birthday — a global celebration of scientific thinking, curiosity, and the theory of evolution that shapes our understanding of the natural world.

dates.name: Earth Day
dates.note: Annual event raising awareness of the natural world and environmental stewardship — connects the Cariboo's wild landscapes to global ecological science.
